Article [3] also reports that "infection and fever rates were the same" with a neutropenic diet as with following usual FDA food-safety guidelines (a normal diet approach).

**Full Answer**: The percentage of patients with infection is the same when comparing a low bacteria diet to a normal diet [1, 3]. Specifically, a low-bacteria diet led to similar infection rates as a normal hospital diet [1]. In children with cancer, infection rates were the same with a neutropenic diet as with following usual FDA food-safety guidelines [3]. Furthermore, avoiding all raw fruits and vegetables (part of a low-bacteria diet) did not lower rates of major infections compared with a diet that allowed them [2].

**Answer**: Same
